adds.rationale: Fills the rockets/spacecraft thematic gap named in the profile as a likely next wave; a six-month-old actively-managed ETF gives the watchlist diversified exposure to launch, satellite-services, and aerospace-enabling-tech names without picking single-stock winners early in the buildup phase.

news_evidence.summary: ARK Invest launches the ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX), an actively-managed fund investing in orbital and suborbital aerospace companies, satellite-services beneficiaries, and enabling-technology firms across robotics, AI, materials, 3D printing, and energy storage.

adds.rationale: Fills the robotics thematic gap; a long-listed, liquid ETF holding global automation and industrial-robotics names is the cheapest way to get early, diversified exposure to the labor-cost-reduction wave the profile names, and current-cycle signals show humanoid-robotics R&D moving from labs to product roadmaps.

news_evidence.summary: Tesla unveils the Tesla Bot humanoid prototype and the Dojo D1 training chip at AI Day, signaling that AI compute and humanoid robotics are now an explicit product roadmap at a megacap manufacturer rather than a research curiosity.
TechCrunch coverage of Tesla AI Day highlights Tesla Bot, Dojo, and the company's plan to repurpose its self-driving AI stack for a general-purpose humanoid robot.

adds.rationale: Fills the nuclear thematic gap via uranium miners; the recently-launched Sprott Physical Uranium Trust is absorbing pounds out of the spot market and lifting uranium prices for the first time in years, an early structural-supply signal that the nuclear-fuel cycle is entering a buildup phase.

news_evidence.summary: Sprott converts the former Uranium Participation Corporation into the Sprott Physical Uranium Trust (inception July 19, 2021), and within weeks the trust's aggressive spot-market buying drives the first sustained uranium-price rally since the post-Fukushima decline.
Uranium spot prices and miner equities rally sharply in September on tight supply and capital flows into uranium ETFs and the Sprott physical trust.

rationale_overall: Day-0 expansion of a thin starter watchlist (AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/SPY/AGG) from five tickers to eight by filling three thematic gaps the profile explicitly named as likely next waves: rockets/spacecraft via ARKX, robotics via BOTZ, and nuclear via URA. All three adds are diversified ETFs rather than single-stock bets, which is the right posture early in a wave's buildup phase when winners are not yet obvious. The AI bucket already has three megacap proxies (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL); no churn there yet. Quantum and engineered-biology buckets are intentionally deferred - quantum pure-plays are still effectively pre-listing or sub-scale, and the profile orders synbio later on the commercial-impact horizon. Total post-change watchlist size is 8 of a 12 cap, leaving room to add single-stock conviction names or fill remaining buckets at subsequent quarterly rebalances.
